_comment: WORKAROUND for LFM2.5 tool calling on a fee-free T4: serve via OLLAMA instead of llama-cpp-python. Ollama ships a prebuilt CUDA runner (no compile), tracks a current llama.cpp (so it has the LFM2.5 tool-parser fix PR #24178), and its OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint returns STRUCTURED tool_calls via Ollama's own template parser — which llama-cpp-python's server cannot do for LFM2's Pythonic <|tool_call_start|>[...]<|tool_call_end|> format. The ollama library tags lfm2.5:8b (= LFM2.5-8B-A1B) with the 'tools' capability. compat.supportsTools=true uses the native tool route. OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH/num_ctx is raised because Ollama caps context small by default. BRAVE_API_KEY is forwarded from ~/.env by the launcher. Run with examples/web_verify_task.json.
